questions.question: A patient needs $0.5$ mg of a medicine per day. If the medicine is available in $0.25$ mg tablets, how many tablets should the patient take daily?
The growth rate of a cell is $25%$ per hour. If there are $100$ cells initially, how many cells will there be after $3$ hours?
What is the decimal equivalent of the fraction $\frac{3}{8}$?
Which of the following rational numbers is greatest: $0.4$, $0.04$, $0.25$, $0.5$, $0.01$?
A medicine bottle contains $500$ ml of liquid. If $0.2$ ml of the medicine is to be given to a patient, what fraction of the total liquid is given?
The probability of a patient recovering from a disease is $80%$. If $50$ patients are treated, how many are expected to recover?
A bacteria population doubles every $2$ hours. If there are $200$ bacteria initially, how many will there be after $6$ hours?
A doctor prescribes a $0.75$ mg dose of a medicine to be taken twice a day. If the patient takes the medicine for $10$ days, what is the total dosage in milligrams? Show your work.

A patient's temperature is $37.2^\circ$C at $8$ am. At $12$ pm, the patient's temperature rises to $38.5^\circ$C. What is the percentage increase in temperature? Use the formula: $\left( \frac{\text{New Value} - \text{Old Value}}{\text{Old Value}} \right) \cdot 100%$. Show your work and round to the nearest whole number.

teacher_rules: For questions 1-8, select the best answer from the given choices.
For questions 9-10, make sure to show all work and calculations.
No explanations or justifications are required for multiple-choice questions.
